hypothesis_en: Mr President, with regard to the Mediterranean and the Middle East, the Council has just recalled the Union's willingness to work with the United States for cooperation in the region. It would also involve realistic and common sense objectives, taking into account, in my view, that excessive ambitions when talking about Morocco to Pakistan can lead to ridicule first and failure afterwards. The United States should accept that it is impossible to deal with such a large-scale enterprise without calling the resolution of the Arab-Israeli conflict a priority and a prior objective. In my opinion, these circumstances, combined with the murder of Hakeem Yassin, have led to the suspension of the new Arab summit scheduled for yesterday in Tunisia, and I believe that no so-called initiative for a so-called Greater Middle East can succeed. In this context, it is enough to show that King Abdullah of Jordan, a moderate leader as we all know, declared yesterday and I quote literally the murder of Yassin a week before the Arab League summit shows that the Israelis wanted the meeting to fail and that the Arabs could not, I insist, If a moderate leader says what I have just read, the Union must take note of what it implies and act accordingly. I would like to ask the Commissioner whether he is aware of the fact that the Commission has already made a number of proposals to the Council and the European Parliament on this matter.
reference_en: thank you very much with regard to the Mediterranean and the Middle East the Council has just said (2.62) that the European Union is prepared to work with the United States with a view to cooperation in the region I think it would be a good job if Washington said the same and expressed its desire to work with the European Union in my view this would lead to the following (1.66) unless they deal with the the settlement of the Arab-Israeli conflict the Saudi peace plan which was adopted in plenary in Beirut by the Arab League has been blocked by Israel and they have also blocked the roadmap they have continued to to plough their own furrow which includes extrajudicial killings in my view these current circumstances especially with the assassination of Sheikh Yassin these have led to the Arab summit planned for Tunis to be suspended (1.28) we need to see this initiative for a greater Middle East ge= getting some success and it's not gonna happen at the moment Rey= Ke= King Abdullah of Jordan a very moderate chap as we all know said yesterday or the day before yesterday the assassination of Sheikh Yassin a week before the Arab League summit shows that the Israelis wanted the meeting to to to fail and Arabs cannot (1.07) relaunch their (1.08) dialogue initiative because Israel does not want peace that is the end of the quotation of what the King of Jordan said if such a moderate leader says what I've just said then the European Union should listen very carefully to what that message is and act in accordance I would conclude President ~re_minding Mr Laschet who is unfortunately not here to listen to me when he says that we should talk less of extrajudicial killings because we all condemn those killings that are against international law oh I would say to him okay fair enough but (1.04) the problem lies in that fact we all condemn it we all condemn them but we don't take any action to ensure that they're stopped thank you very much
source_es: Presidente refiriéndose al Mediterráneo y a Oriente Próximo el Consejo acaba de recordar (1.92) la disposición cito textualmente la disposición de la Unión a trabajar con los Estados Unidos a efectos de la cooperación en la región no estaría mal digo yo que Washington hiciera lo mismo y expresara su ferviente deseo de cooperar con la Unión (1.08) en mi opinión esto implicaría lo siguiente una presentación y elaboración sutil no descarnada y brutal de su supuesto plan para la democratización de Oriente Medio implicaría también objetivos ajustados a la realidad y al sentido común (1.63) teniendo en cuenta desde mi punto de vista que las ambiciones desmedidas cuando se habla de Marruecos a Pakistán pueden conducir al ridículo primero y al fracaso después y sobre todo en mi opinión los Estados Unidos deberían aceptar que es imposible hacer frente a una empresa de tal envergadura sin calificar de objetivo prioritario y previo la resolución del conflicto árabe-israelí Israel ha despreciado la propuesta de paz saudí refrendada en su día en Beirut por la Liga Árabe en pleno al igual que ha bloqueado y no debemos engañarnos insisto ha bloqueado la hoja de ruta y ha decidido continuar su propia vía unilateral que comprende los asesinatos extrajudiciales (1.03) en mi opinión insisto estas circunstancias unidas al asesinato del Jeque Yassin han provocado la suspensión de la nueva Cumbre Árabe prevista para ayer en Túnez y creo que ninguna supuesta iniciativa para un así llamado Gran Oriente Medio puede tener éxito alguno en este contexto baste como muestra que el rey Abdalá de Jordania dirigente manifiestamente moderado como sabemos todos haya declarado anteayer y cito textualmente el asesinato de Yassin una semana antes de la Cumbre de la Liga Árabe demuestra que los israelíes deseaban que la reunión fracasara y que los árabes no puedan relanzar insisto no puedan relanzar su iniciativa de liado= de diálogo porque Israel no quiere la paz fin de la cita del rey Abdalá de Jordania si un líder moderado dice esto que acabo de leer la Unión debe tomar nota de lo que ello implica y actuar en consecuencia y termino señor Presidente diciendo recordando al colega Laschet que acaba de expresarse aunque lamento que no esté aquí que cuando se refiere y dice que debemos hablar menos de los asesinatos extrajudiciales porque todos condenamos esos asesinatos que son contrarios al derecho internacional (1.02) digo yo estoy de acuerdo con eso el problema radica en que precisamente sucede eso que todos los condenamos pero no actuamos ni unos ni otros para que sean definitivamente evitados muchas gracias Presidente
